extraction.one_line_summary: EWAN Caleb won stage 3 sprinting from ACKERMANN Pascal's wheel after LOTTO SOUDAL controlled the entire finale in Scherpenheuvel.
tactical_patterns: LOTTO SOUDAL positioned EWAN on ACKERMANN's wheel rather than leading out, exploiting rival leadout train.
VAN POPPEL Danny launched early sprint that was easily matched, revealing premature timing cost position.
EVENEPOEL Remco maintains 45-second gap on teammate without defending, suggesting team comfortable with passive GC tactics.

rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: positive: winning from ACKERMANN's wheel shows tactical maturity to steal rival leadout rather than force own train
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: strong leadout work resulted in only second place, team doing the work for EWAN's benefit
rider_signals.MØRKØV Michael: neutral: third place shows individual speed but lack of podium suggests leadout timing issues
rider_signals.VAN POPPEL Danny: negative: early sprint attempt easily matched by EWAN reveals poor timing judgment in finale
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: 45-second GC lead over teammate suggests dominant position without needing to actively defend
